content: Climeworks uses solid-sorbent direct air capture (DAC): modular collectors pull CO₂ from ambient air, then heat and/or vacuum release it for permanent geological storage. A key 2024 milestone was inaugurating Mammoth in Iceland, currently the world’s largest DAC plant, designed to capture up to 36,000 tons of CO₂ per year and already delivering Puro Standard–certified carbon removal. Another major step was the public reveal of its Generation 3 DAC technology, which uses novel structured sorbents to roughly halve energy use per ton versus Gen 2 and underpins the planned megaton-scale Project Cypress hub in Louisiana.
